title: Question: Using enumerated values?
body:
---


Author Name: **Robert Baruch** (@RobertBaruch)
Original Redmine Message: 2064 from https://www.veripool.org



---

I have a file with an enum typedef:

```
package common;

typedef enum bit[1:0] { NONE, RST, INC, LOAD } microaddr_cmd;

endpackage: common

```

After compiling this in with my main module, it seems there is no way to refer to this enum in C++. I'd like to use the enum values in my test code, because having to figure out which enum corresponds to which bit pattern is kind of the thing that humans shouldn't have to do and computers are good at. Maybe not for the above simple enum, but it can quickly get out of hand (see: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cognitive_load)

Is there any way to generate C++ enums for SystemVerilog enums?
